hero.motto: Leading to excellence in Torah-Zionist education.

about.lede: Ulpenat Tzvia Kokhav Ya'akov is a religious-Zionist ulpana for young women — a community where the beit midrash, the lab, and the studio share the same blueprint.
about.body1: Founded in 1998 in Kokhav Ya'akov, Tzvia has grown into one of the leading religious-Zionist ulpanot in Mateh Binyamin. Our educational philosophy integrates deep Torah study with academic excellence and personal development.
about.body2: Our faculty brings together outstanding women scholars in Torah, leading scientists, and experienced educators — creating an environment where each student is challenged and supported to reach her highest potential.
about.body3: Graduates go on to national service, higher education, and careers in every field, carrying with them a life rooted in Torah and a mind sharpened by years of serious study.
about.pullQuote: We don't teach our students to choose between Torah and the world — we teach them that the world is where Torah is studied most honestly.

items.title: New STEM wing breaks ground next semester
items.excerpt: A 1,200 sqm addition with new labs, a makerspace, and a dedicated robotics studio.

items.title: Tenth grade takes national Tanakh quiz
items.excerpt: For the second consecutive year, our team brought home the regional title.

items.title: Open House — June 4th, 17:00
items.excerpt: Prospective students and families are welcome to tour campus and meet faculty.

items.title: Spotlight — Dr. R. Cohen on neuroscience
items.excerpt: From Tzvia to Tel Aviv University — research, family, meaning.

academy.intro: Life at Tzvia extends far beyond the classroom — a full programme of activities, journeys, and disciplines that shape each student as a whole person.

activities.name: Land of Israel
activities.desc: Jerusalem, the Negev, and the mountain region explored through history, geography, nature, and research.

activities.name: Theatre
activities.desc: A three-year track combining dramatic literature, acting, directing, and stagecraft.

activities.name: Science & Technology
activities.desc: Biology, physics, and chemistry — lab-based and online tracks that build scientific thinking.

activities.name: Basketball
activities.desc: Movement, teamwork, and confidence on the large court at the center of campus.

activities.name: Social Sciences
activities.desc: Psychology, sociology, and research tools for understanding people and society.

activities.name: Jewish Thought
activities.desc: Tradition, identity, values, and meaningful questions explored in depth.

activities.name: Visual Arts
activities.desc: Creativity in action — painting, drawing, and design that enrich school life.

events.title: Last Day of Classes
events.desc: The final day of classes for the 5786 school year.

contact.h3: Come visit us on campus, or send a note.
contact.lede: We're a 25-minute drive from Jerusalem. Campus tours run Tuesdays and Thursdays — sign up below or write the office directly.
contact.placeholderMessage: Tell us a little about yourself or your daughter — and how we can help.

footer.brandDesc: A religious-Zionist ulpana for young women at the heart of Mateh Binyamin. Twenty-eight years of scholarship, community, and quiet excellence.
footer.newsletterHint: A quarterly note from the head of school — community, scholarship, and what's next.

academyPage.intro: A full programme of activities, academic disciplines, and enrichment tracks — designed to develop each student as a whole person.
academyPage.advisorDesc: Each programme has different commitments and entry points. We can help you find the right fit.

categories.label: ARTS
categories.desc: Stage, canvas, and instrument — disciplines where attention becomes craft.

categories.label: SCIENCE & TECH
categories.desc: Laboratories, makerspace, and online tracks — inquiry as a daily discipline.

categories.label: SPORTS
categories.desc: Team sports, conditioning, and individual disciplines across all grades.

categories.label: HUMANITIES
categories.desc: Text, history, and thought — the disciplines that shape how we read everything else.

aboutPage.heroDesc: A religious-Zionist girls' high school in the heart of Mateh Binyamin — where Torah study, academic excellence, and love of the land meet.

facts.label: Academic Tracks
facts.value: Land of Israel Studies · Biology · Chemistry · Social Sciences · Theatre

facts.label: Daily Transport
facts.value: 6 subsidized routes — Beit El · Psagot · Michmas · Kochav HaShachar · Har Homa · Gilo

philosophyBody: Ulpenat Tzvia Kochav Ya'akov aspires to educate young women of yirat shamayim — God-fearing women of good character, leaders of halakha guided by Torah and faith, conscious of their role in the generation of redemption.

Students participate in shaping the people and the land. A meticulous emphasis on the precise observance of mitzvot, alongside the spirit of halakha, is one of the foundational characteristics of the ulpana.
The ulpana is a warm home — supportive and embracing — for students navigating the years of adolescence, with an emphasis on building personal relationships between staff and students.
aboutPage.philosophyQuote: We don't teach our students to choose between Torah and the world — we teach them that the world is where Torah is studied most honestly.

leaders.name: Tehila Biti
leaders.bio: Tehila Biti has led Ulpenat Tzvia for over a decade, building one of the most respected Torah-Zionist educational communities in Mateh Binyamin.

leaders.name: Uria Mizrahi
leaders.bio: Uria Mizrahi oversees the academic program and matriculation tracks, partnering with staff to keep teaching rigorous and forward-looking.

leaders.name: Miriam Cohen
leaders.bio: Miriam Cohen guides the beit midrash and kodesh curriculum, weaving daily Torah study into the rhythm of school life.

leaders.name: Chaya Levi
leaders.bio: Chaya Levi leads the social and dormitory programs, building the warm, embracing home the ulpana is known for.

leaders.name: Batya Shapira
leaders.bio: Batya Shapira coordinates the specialization tracks, from Land of Israel studies to the sciences and theatre.

specs.name: Land of Israel Studies
specs.desc: Deep exploration of the geography, archaeology, and history of the land of Israel.

specs.name: Biology
specs.desc: Laboratory-based science track leading to full matriculation.

specs.name: Chemistry
specs.desc: Advanced chemistry track with laboratory practice and research methodology.

specs.name: Social Sciences
specs.desc: Sociology, psychology, and civic studies that shape how students read society.

specs.name: Theatre
specs.desc: Acting, directing, and stagecraft culminating in an annual production.

milestones.title: Founding
milestones.desc: Ulpenat Tzvia Kochav Ya'akov opens its doors for the first time.

milestones.title: First Decade
milestones.desc: The school reaches full enrollment across all four grade levels and adds its first specialisation track.

milestones.title: New Campus Wing
milestones.desc: A major expansion adds new classrooms, a science laboratory, and beit midrash facilities.

milestones.title: Today
milestones.desc: 233 students across grades 9–12, five academic tracks, and a vibrant community rooted in Torah and Zionism.

newsArchive.desc: Announcements, achievements, events, and alumnae spotlights — the full archive of life at Tzvia.

admissionsPage.heroDesc: We warmly invite eighth-grade students and their families to explore what Ulpenat Tzvia has to offer. Our admissions process is designed to be clear, respectful, and welcoming.

steps.title: Family Meetings.
steps.desc: An initial meeting between the family and the admissions team to learn about the school, its values, and the student's background.

steps.title: Submit via the Chemed Portal.
steps.desc: All applications are processed through the Ministry of Education's Chemed registration system. Submissions are open from January 21st to February 19th.

steps.title: Campus Assessment Day.
steps.desc: Invited students spend a day at the ulpana — attending classes, meeting staff and students, and participating in a short structured assessment.

steps.title: Receive Your Response.
steps.desc: Acceptance notifications are sent out from March 9th at 16:00 via the Chemed system and by direct contact from the school.

steps.title: Final Confirmation.
steps.desc: Accepted families confirm their place by March 16th. The admissions team remains available for any questions throughout this period.

admissionsPage.openHouseDesc: Join us for one of our two open house events — each tailored to students from different regions.

openHouseDays.title: Binyamin Region
openHouseDays.desc: Open house for students from Mateh Binyamin settlements and surrounding communities.

openHouseDays.title: Jerusalem & Surroundings
openHouseDays.desc: Open house for students from Jerusalem and the greater metropolitan area.

resources.title: Questions?
resources.desc: Our admissions team is available to answer any questions about the process, the school, or student life at Tzvia.

resources.title: Forms & Documents
resources.desc: Download required forms, read the school prospectus, and access supplementary materials for the application process.

admissionsPage.ctaEyebrow: WE LOOK FORWARD TO MEETING YOU

rentPage.heroDesc2: The Ulpenat Tzvia Kochav Ya'akov campus offers a quiet corner in Binyamin, a short drive from Jerusalem. Green open spaces, new and air-conditioned dormitory rooms, and a full dining hall.
